settings.title: Ten Years of Keeping Local Businesses Running
settings.excerpt: A decade in, we are marking the milestone by donating refurbished kit to community groups nearby.
settings.body:
Uplink started ten years ago with two engineers, a van, and a handful of clients who took a chance on us. Today we look after dozens of local businesses, but the job has not really changed. People want their technology to simply work.

Ten years of quiet mornings

In the early days the two of us did everything, from setting up a single office printer to talking a worried owner through a crash over the phone. The tools have moved on a great deal since then, but the heart of the work is the same. A business calls us because they would rather think about their customers than their computers, and our job is to let them.

A few things have stayed true across the decade.

  • We still answer the phone ourselves when you call
  • We still treat a small firm's problem as seriously as a large one
  • We still measure a good day by how little you had to think about us

Marking it by giving back

To mark the decade, we are refurbishing computers retired from client upgrades and donating them to community groups in the area. Hardware that still has years of life left should be helping someone, not sitting in storage.

It seemed a fitting way to mark ten years. The same machines that kept a local business running can now help a community group do its work, and that feels far better than seeing them gather dust.

Thank you to every client who has trusted us with the systems their business depends on. Here is to ten more years of quiet, reliable mornings.
